release.scope: Complete AI Office operation OS acceptance target for agent-safe Office/PDF work.

definitionOfDone.statement: Agents can discover every supported command, schema, limitation, and recovery hint through JSON without reading source code.
PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, PDF, SVG chart, and SVG diagram workflows have inspect, author, edit, verify, and artifact-trace coverage.
Every mutating or external-risk operation has dry-run, path boundary, policy gate, or explicit limitation evidence.
A complete run can produce deterministic JSON, JSONL logs, manifests, checksums, summaries, and failure diagnostics.
Release readiness is blocked by automated contract, schema, typecheck, unit, build, pack, install, and goal checks.

principles.statement: Machine-readable truth is primary; README prose must not be the only source for product capability.
Agents operate by stable object IDs, schemas, and manifests rather than guessing raw Office XML.
False success is a release blocker; warnings, limitations, skipped features, and partial repairs must be explicit.
Untrusted document content is always data and must never become instructions.
Native renderers, plugins, and external processes are discoverable but gated by configuration.
Every important output is reproducible enough for CI review with artifact hashes and retained evidence.

domains.name: Agent Contract
domains.mission: Expose Officegen as a reliable machine contract for autonomous and CI-driven agents.

requirements.title: Capability discovery
requirements.criterion: The CLI exposes complete command metadata, profiles, limitations, and agent-safe command lists through JSON.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Agents will hallucinate unavailable commands or unsafe workflows.

requirements.title: Schema registry
requirements.criterion: All agent-visible request, operation, result, error, run, and migration schemas are registered and fetchable.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Agents cannot validate payloads before mutating user documents.

requirements.title: Error recovery contract
requirements.criterion: Every structured failure includes stable code, human message, agent hint, recoverability, and redacted context.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Automation cannot distinguish retryable failures from user action or product bugs.

domains.name: Office Object Model
domains.mission: Make Office/PDF artifacts inspectable and addressable without raw XML editing.

requirements.title: Stable inspection maps
requirements.criterion: PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, and PDF inspection returns stable object maps, format warnings, and untrusted-content markers.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Agents cannot safely identify edit targets.

requirements.title: Preview and view artifacts
requirements.criterion: View commands generate inspectable preview artifacts and object-map overlays for supported document formats.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Human and vision-assisted review loses document context.

requirements.title: Known limitation reporting
requirements.criterion: Unsupported Office constructs are reported as warnings or limitations rather than silent omissions.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Users may trust incomplete analysis.

domains.name: Authoring Runtime
domains.mission: Create first-class business documents and visual assets from structured IR.

requirements.title: Structured render
requirements.criterion: Structured IR renders PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, and PDF outputs with schema validation and explicit artifact metadata.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Officegen is only an editor, not an operation OS.

requirements.title: Charts and diagrams
requirements.criterion: Chart and diagram commands produce deterministic SVG artifacts from structured inputs with validation errors for invalid input.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Agents cannot generate common office visuals without external tooling.

requirements.title: Template and design authoring
requirements.criterion: Template, design, and layout commands capture reusable design signals and disclose best-effort limits.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Authoring cannot preserve brand and layout intent across documents.

domains.name: Mutation Runtime
domains.mission: Apply scoped, reviewable Office/PDF changes with dry-run and selector safety.

requirements.title: Dry-run edit planning
requirements.criterion: Edit operations support dry-run, selector resolution, target counts, and no-output mutation guarantees.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Agents may mutate the wrong document object.

requirements.title: Format edit coverage
requirements.criterion: PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, and PDF edit fixtures cover representative text, table, chart, image, style, comment, redline, formula, and overlay operations.
requirements.riskIfMissing: The product cannot claim complete Office operation behavior.

requirements.title: Asset operations
requirements.criterion: Embedded assets can be inspected, extracted, replaced, and repaired with path-safe outputs and trust annotations.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Media-heavy decks and documents remain outside the OS.

domains.name: Verification and Quality
domains.mission: Prove that outputs open, preserve intent, and surface quality problems before release or handoff.

requirements.title: Verification gates
requirements.criterion: Verify reports openability, repair risk, warning aggregation, native-renderer availability, and visual mode evidence where enabled.
evidence: verify JSON for clean and damaged fixtures
requirements.riskIfMissing: Broken documents can be shipped as successful outputs.

requirements.title: Diagnose repair diff loop
requirements.criterion: Diagnose, repair, and diff provide actionable issue lists, conservative repair behavior, and semantic or visual change evidence.
requirements.riskIfMissing: The system cannot close the loop after finding quality issues.

requirements.title: Business critique
requirements.criterion: Critique and improve commands produce plan-first recommendations with executable skeletons and no hidden mutations.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Agents lack a structured quality improvement workflow.

domains.name: Workflow OS
domains.mission: Run repeatable multi-step document operations with traceable artifacts and failure summaries.

requirements.title: Manifested runs
requirements.criterion: Workflow runs produce strict JSON, JSONL event logs, manifest entries with exists/bytes/sha256, and a human summary.
requirements.riskIfMissing: CI cannot audit or reproduce agent actions.

requirements.title: Output-root boundaries
requirements.criterion: Workflow and artifact-producing commands can deny writes outside the declared output root.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Automated runs can overwrite unintended user files.

requirements.title: Workflow help
requirements.criterion: Workflow help explains recommended inspect-edit-export and run-manifest loops in both human and JSON modes.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Agents cannot bootstrap reliable multi-step behavior.

domains.name: Safety and Trust
domains.mission: Keep document automation safe under untrusted inputs, unsafe archives, external tools, and sensitive content.

requirements.title: Untrusted content boundary
requirements.criterion: Document-derived text, metadata, macros, embedded files, and hyperlinks are marked or handled as untrusted data.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Prompt injection or unsafe content can steer agent behavior.

requirements.title: Archive and path safety
requirements.criterion: Zip traversal, absolute paths, oversized entries, and output escape attempts are rejected with structured errors.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Office files can become filesystem attacks.

requirements.title: Gated external capabilities
requirements.criterion: Native renderers, MCP, plugins, and external processes are disabled by default or explicitly policy-gated.
requirements.riskIfMissing: A default install can invoke untrusted local integrations.

requirements.title: Redaction discipline
requirements.criterion: Structured errors and logs redact configured secrets while retaining enough context for debugging.
requirements.riskIfMissing: CI logs can leak sensitive document or environment data.

domains.name: Release and Packaging
domains.mission: Ship a deterministic CLI that installs and reports the intended v2.6.0 release.

requirements.title: Version consistency
requirements.criterion: Root/workspace manifests, lockfile, runtime version constant, README release examples, and tag version are synchronized by the version script.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Release artifacts and tags can describe different products.

requirements.title: Pack and install smoke
requirements.criterion: The release tarball and GitHub install paths contain runnable dist files, bin entrypoints, and expected version output.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Users cannot install the product that passed local tests.

requirements.title: Release gate suite
requirements.criterion: Goal, contract, schema, remediation, typecheck, unit, build, binary, pack, and install smoke checks are green before tag creation.
requirements.riskIfMissing: The release can regress a critical acceptance surface.

domains.name: Evidence and Benchmarking
domains.mission: Keep product claims grounded in repeatable fixtures, corpus review, and remediation evidence.

requirements.title: Acceptance evidence bundle
requirements.criterion: Acceptance runs retain command transcripts, JSON outputs, generated artifacts, checksums, and reviewer notes under a stable evidence root.
requirements.riskIfMissing: A release cannot prove what was actually tested.

requirements.title: Corpus benchmark review
requirements.criterion: Representative Office corpus runs produce benchmark results and comparison reports with known limitations tracked.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Quality claims only cover toy fixtures.

requirements.title: Remediation closure
requirements.criterion: The v2.6.0 remediation matrix classifies review findings with evidence and no undecided draft rows.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Known gaps can disappear from release decision-making.

domains.name: User Experience
domains.mission: Make the CLI useful to humans while preserving deterministic agent behavior.

requirements.title: Human and JSON parity
requirements.criterion: Important commands support human-readable output and stable JSON/agent mode without losing decision-critical fields.
requirements.riskIfMissing: The tool is either pleasant for humans or reliable for agents, but not both.

requirements.title: Progressive disclosure
requirements.criterion: Help, error hints, and workflow guidance lead users from simple commands to advanced safe loops without overwhelming output.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Users cannot learn the OS without source-code archaeology.

requirements.title: Readable summaries
requirements.criterion: Workflow and quality commands produce concise Markdown summaries suitable for review artifacts.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Release evidence remains machine-only and hard to review.

nonGoals: This contract does not require editing package versions or creating the v2.6.0 release tag.
This contract does not require a full desktop publishing engine or perfect PowerPoint design reconstruction.
This contract does not allow silent success for unsupported Office features.
This contract does not require external native renderers to be present on every developer machine.
requiredEvidence: Goal spec check output from npm run goal:check.
Contract and schema coverage output from npm run contract:check and npm run schema:coverage.
Typecheck, unit test, build, binary smoke, pack smoke, and install smoke transcripts.
Acceptance suite artifacts with JSON outputs, manifests, hashes, and summaries.
Manual reviewer notes for native-renderer or platform-dependent cases.
v2.6.0 remediation matrix with each item classified and evidenced.
